level1_segments.summary: The head surgeon performs a series of drilling and tissue manipulation tasks on the patient with brief pauses in between.
The surgeon calibrates the robotic arm, cuts the patient, and hammers the surgical site with intermittent pauses.
The head surgeon engages in an extended session of calibrating and manipulating the Mako robotic arm and bone saw.
The head surgeon calibrates and manipulates the Mako robotic arm while preparing the bone saw for use.
The head surgeon uses the bone saw to cut the patient's bone under robotic guidance.
The head surgeon pauses briefly before touching and hammering the patient to adjust the surgical site.
After a long pause, the head surgeon prepares the instrument table and handles surgical instruments.
The head surgeon applies cement to the patient, hammers the site, and touches the patient to secure the implant.
Following a brief pause, the head surgeon sutures the patient while holding surgical instruments.
The head surgeon drills into the patient's bone using a handheld drill.
The head surgeon remains idle with no active interactions for an extended period.

level2_segments.summary: The head surgeon performs robotic calibration and executes guided bone cutting procedures.
The surgeon adjusts the surgical site, applies cement to secure the implant, performs suturing, and concludes with final drilling and idle time.

level1_segments.summary: The robot monitor displays the Bone Registration phase of the procedure.
The system transitions to the Intra OP Planning stage.
The surgeon performs iterative bone preparation and RIO checks across anterior, posterior, and distal surfaces with brief pauses between steps.
The system returns to Intra OP Planning for final adjustments.
The procedure concludes as the robot monitor displays the Finish state.

level2_segments.summary: The robot monitor displays the initial Bone Registration phase of the procedure.
